channel.title: Bloomberg Technology
channel.copyright: Copyright 2025 BLOOMBERG L.P. ALL RIGHTS RESERVED

item.title: AMD Inks Chip Deal With OpenAI That Triggers Explosive Rally
item.description: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. landed a blockbuster deal with OpenAI to build artificial intelligence infrastructure, giving the chipmaker a chance to show it can mount a challenge to Nvidia Corp. in the AI computing industry.
item.dc:creator: Ian King

media:content.media:description: Sam Altman and AMD CEO Lisa Su in Washington, DC, in May. Photographer: Nathan Howard/Bloomberg

item.title: Hedge Funds and Crypto Lure Gulf Heirs Set to Inherit $1 Trillion
item.description: In 2020, two young brothers at a 135-year-old Gulf merchant family approached one of its money-managers with an idea: bet on Bitcoin.
item.dc:creator: Laura Gardner Cuesta, Hema Parmar, Nicolas Parasie, Alexander Sazonov

media:content.media:description: Dubai’s financial hub has become home to more than 70 hedge funds.

item.title: AppLovin Probed by SEC Over Its Data-Collection Practices
item.description: The Securities and Exchange Commission has been probing the data-collection practices of the mobile advertising tech company AppLovin Corp., according to people familiar with the matter. The company’s shares slid.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Solon

media:content.media:description: The AppLovin logo.

item.title: Citi’s Montagu Warns US Tech Rally at Risk From Profit Taking
item.description: US technology stocks face growing risks as investors seek to cash in on this year’s record-breaking rally, according to Citigroup Inc. strategists.
item.dc:creator: Julien Ponthus

media:content.media:description: The Nasdaq 100 set its latest record on Monday after AMD's blockbuster deal with OpenAI. Photographer: Annabelle Chih/Bloomberg

item.title: Jaguar Land Rover to Restart Output in UK After Cyberattack
item.description: Jaguar Land Rover Automotive Plc will resume production at some of its UK sites after weeks-long outages following a cyberattack that snarled its global manufacturing network.
item.dc:creator: William Wilkes

media:content.media:description: Range Rover production at the Jaguar Land Rover plant in Solihull, UK. Photographer: Chris Ratcliffe/Bloomberg

item.title: Telefonica Seeks Leaner, Bolder Structure Under Chief’s Review
item.description: An executive shake-up, potential job cuts and a pledge to do more deals all offer glimpses into the Telefonica SA chairman’s ongoing strategic review, set to be presented next month with the ambition of kick starting growth at the Spanish carrier.
item.dc:creator: Rodrigo Orihuela, Clara Hernanz Lizarraga

media:content.media:description: Marc Murtra Photographer: Angel Garcia/Bloomberg

item.title: EU Mulls Sanctions Against Russia-Linked Stablecoin A7A5
item.description: The European Union has proposed imposing sanctions on A7A5, a ruble-backed stablecoin tied to sanctioned Russian actors, according to documents seen by Bloomberg News.
item.dc:creator: Alberto Nardelli, Emily Nicolle

media:content.media:description: Ilan Shor Photographer: Irina Buzhor/Sipa/AP Photo

item.title: LG Electronics India Starts Taking Bids for $1.3 Billion IPO
item.description: LG Electronics Inc.’s Indian unit started taking orders for its $1.3 billion initial public offering on Tuesday, joining Tata Capital Ltd. to launch deals in what could be a record month for new listings in the country.
item.dc:creator: Ashutosh Joshi

item.title: Elon Musk Names Ex-Morgan Stanley Banker CFO of xAI, FT Reports
item.description: Elon Musk named a former Morgan Stanley executive the chief financial officer of xAI, the Financial Times reported, appointing an ally to help oversee one of the largest US artificial intelligence startups.
item.dc:creator: Edwin Chan

media:content.media:description: xAI. Photographer: Gabby Jones/Bloomberg

item.title: Dubai’s Alleged Crypto Scams Are Raking in Billions (Correct)
item.description: Ponzi and pyramid schemes dressed in startup clothes have been running rampant in the UAE, experts and US authorities say.
item.dc:creator: Alice Kantor

item.title: OpenAI Lets ChatGPT Users Connect With Spotify, Zillow In App
item.description: OpenAI is making it easier for ChatGPT users to connect with third-party apps within the chatbot to carry out tasks, the company’s latest bid to turn its flagship product into a key gateway for digital services.
item.dc:creator: Shirin Ghaffary, Rachel Metz

media:content.media:description: OpenAI signage displayed outside the venue for their news conference in Seoul, South Korea, on Wednesday, Sept, 10, 2025. OpenAI established its third Asian office in South Korea, its largest market in the Asia Pacific region based on paid subscription.

item.title: Qualtrics Agrees to Buy Press Ganey Forsta in $6.75 Billion Deal
item.description: Qualtrics International Inc. agreed to buy health-care survey firm Press Ganey Forsta in a deal valued at $6.75 billion, in the latest private equity-driven acquisition of an enterprise software business.
item.dc:creator: Christina Kyriasoglou

media:content.media:description: The Qualtrics International Inc. website on a smartphone. Photographer: Gabby Jones/Bloomberg

item.title: Supreme Court Denies Google’s Bid to Pause App Store Changes
item.description: Alphabet Inc.’s Google lost a US Supreme Court bid to pause major changes to its Google Play app store in an antitrust case filed by Fortnite-maker Epic Games Inc.
item.dc:creator: Josh Sisco, Greg Stohr

media:content.media:description: The Google Play booth during the Tokyo Game Show 2024 in Chiba, Japan. Photographer: Tomohiro Ohsumi/Getty Images AsiaPac

item.title: AMD's Chip Deal With OpenAI Triggers Explosive Rally | Bloomberg Tech 10/6/2025
item.description: AMD CEO Lisa Su and OpenAI President Greg Brockman join Bloomberg Tech to weigh in on the two companies' deal to roll out AI infrastructure, a pact the chipmaker said could generate tens of billions of dollars in new revenue. Plus, White House AI and Crypto Czar David Sacks defends the Trump administration’s approach to China in the context of the global AI race, and Meta CMO Alex Schultz has a new book on how AI is changing the advertising landscape. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: OpenAI’s Golden Touch Spreads as Stocks Soar
item.description: OpenAI has already proved it has a golden touch when it comes to partnering with other tech firms to deploy its artificial intelligence products. Its power to move shares, though, is rapidly spreading to companies it even briefly discusses teaming up with.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Vlastelica

item.title: Verizon Replaces CEO With New Chief Promising to Restore Growth
item.description: Verizon Communications Inc., conceding it has fallen behind rivals in wireless and broadband service, replaced its chief executive officer of seven years, appointing a new leader with experience in telecom and financial technology.
item.dc:creator: Kelcee Griffis

media:content.media:description: Dan Schulman Photographer: Bryan van der Beek/Bloomberg

item.title: Entergy in Talks With Hyperscalers on Big Louisiana Data Centers
item.description: Multiple technology companies are seeking to build large data centers in Northern Louisiana after Meta Platforms Inc. announced plans to construct its largest AI-focused facility in the region, according to Entergy Louisiana Chief Executive Officer Phillip May.
item.dc:creator: Josh Saul, Riley Griffin

media:content.media:description: Entergy signage outside their headquarters, Tuesday, May 7, 2024, in New Orleans.

item.title: OpenAI Is Fast Becoming a Whale in Stock Market It Has Shunned
item.description: OpenAI may not be publicly traded, but the world’s most valuable startup is increasingly making waves in the stock market.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Vlastelica

item.title: Bitcoin’s Record Rally Fueled by Growing Bets in Options Markets
item.description: Bitcoin set a fresh record on Sunday for the first time since mid-August, as the US government shutdown drove investors to safe-haven assets in a migration dubbed the “debasement trade.”
item.dc:creator: Suvashree Ghosh, Sidhartha Shukla

item.title: US Bond Traders Are Scouring Shutdown Betting Odds in Data Void
item.description: Treasury traders are turning to betting markets for signals on the length of the US government shutdown in the absence of economic data to guide interest rate expectations.
item.dc:creator: Liz Capo McCormick

item.title: Supreme Court Rejects Bid to Sue Meta Over Church Shooting
item.description: The US Supreme Court declined a chance to open social media companies to lawsuits over content recommended by their algorithms, turning away an appeal that accused Meta Platforms Inc.’s Facebook of radicalizing a man who killed nine South Carolina churchgoers.
item.dc:creator: Greg Stohr

media:content.media:description: Photographs of the nine victims killed at the Emanuel African Methodist Episcopal Church in Charleston, South Carolina during a prayer vigil at the the Metropolitan AME Church in Washington in 2015.

item.title: Market Only Looking at Positives of AMD, OpenAI Deal, Says Analyst Goldberg
item.description: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. signed a deal with OpenAI for AI infrastructure that could generate tens of billions of dollars in new revenue over multiple years. Jay Goldberg, senior analyst at Seaport, examines the deal on “Bloomberg Surveillance.” (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: TikTok Averts Indonesia Shutdown After Sharing Streaming Data
item.description: TikTok shared a trove of data about Indonesian users’ online activity with the country’s government over the weekend, handing over potentially sensitive information to avert a license suspension in its biggest Southeast Asian market.
item.dc:creator: Prima Wirayani, Olivia Poh

media:content.media:description: The TikTok app page in the Google Play store on a laptop arranged in the Queens borough of New York, US, on Wednesday, Jan. 15, 2025. The Supreme Court signaled it's likely to uphold a law that would ban the popular TikTok social media platform in the US if it isn't sold by its Chinese parent company by Jan. 19. Photographer: Gabby Jones/Bloomberg

item.title: FIFA’s Blockchain-Based Ticketing System for 2026 World Cup Faces Preliminary Probe
item.description: Switzerland’s gambling regulator has opened a preliminary probe into sales of blockchain-based tokens backed by FIFA which fans can exchange for tickets to games at the 2026 World Cup.
item.dc:creator: Emily Nicolle, Giles Turner

media:content.media:description: The Fifa World Cup trophy Photographer: Fabrice Coffrini/AFP/Getty Images

item.title: Online Holiday Spending Expected to Rise 5.3%, Fueled by Debt
item.description: US shoppers will spend $253.4 billion online in November and December, up 5.3% from 2024, according to Adobe Inc., with “buy now pay later” checkout options fueling growth.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Soper

media:content.media:description: A woman uses a credit card for online shopping. Photographer: Hollie Adams/Bloomberg

item.title: Apple Faces Probe in France Over Voice Recordings Made by Siri
item.description: Apple Inc. faces an investigation in France over the use of voice recordings made with its assistant Siri.
item.dc:creator: Benoit Berthelot, Gaspard Sebag

media:content.media:description: This photograph shows wordmark of Siri, a digital assistant developed by Apple Inc., displayed on a smartphone in Bordeaux, south-western France on February 14, 2025. (Photo by Philippe LOPEZ / AFP) (Photo by PHILIPPE LOPEZ/AFP via Getty Images)

item.title: Big Debt Deals Throw Fuel on the AI Boom
item.description: Welcome to Tech In Depth, our daily newsletter about the business of tech from Bloomberg’s journalists around the world. Today, Ed Ludlow reports on the financing that is helping fuel the AI boom.
item.dc:creator: Edward Ludlow

media:content.media:description: Signage is displayed at the cloud infrastructure department of an Oracle Corp. office in Seattle, Washington, U.S., on Thursday, July 19, 2018. Microsoft Corp. and Oracle Corp. announced a cloud interoperability partnership enabling customers to migrate and run mission-critical enterprise workloads across Microsoft Azure and Oracle Cloud.

item.title: Telefonica Chile Bonds Whipsawed by News After Months of Silence
item.description: Bonds in Telefonica Moviles Chile SA nosedived at the end of last month on fears the company was about to be broken up and sold, then rebounded when it was revealed that the names behind one potential bid are two of Latin America’s largest telecom firms.
item.dc:creator: Carolina Gonzalez, Valentina Fuentes

media:content.media:description: A Movistar mobile phone service store in Santiago, Chile, on Monday, May 19, 2025. Bonds of Telefonica Moviles Chile SA slumped into distressed territory last week on a report that its parent company was about to throw in the towel, selling the asset after years of fierce competition in the local mobile phone market.

item.title: EQT Invests in Legal Startup Harvey to Fuel International Growth
item.description: Legal services platform Harvey has raised €50 million ($59 million) in a strategic investment from an EQT AB fund to support the company’s international expansion.
item.dc:creator: Michael Hytha

media:content.media:description: LONDON, ENGLAND - JANUARY 19: A statue of the Scales of Justice stands above the Old Bailey on January 19, 2021 in London, England. Criminal watchdogs representing England and Wales have expressed concern over the backlog of cases, caused by the Coronavirus pandemic. Figures have revealed that the backlog of unheard cases in the crown courts has reached 54,000. (Photo by Dan Kitwood/Getty Images) Photographer: Dan Kitwood/Getty Images Europe

item.title: Blackstone, Abu Dhabi’s Lunate Team Up on $5 Billion Logistics Platform
item.description: Blackstone Inc. is partnering with Abu Dhabi’s Lunate on a new platform that will target investments worth $5 billion across the wider region, as the US private equity giant looks to build on its portfolio of logistics assets.
item.dc:creator: Dinesh Nair

media:content.media:description: Warehousing.
